dataset.name: EID Quick Reference - Plug and Play / Device Installation

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.notice: Event descriptions are paraphrased summaries written for this dataset. Source links point to authoritative references.

entries.title: Device driver successfully installed
entries.summary: Windows successfully installed device drivers for a new hardware device.
entries.details: Generated in the System event log by the Microsoft-Windows-UserPnP provider when Windows completes the installation of drivers for a newly connected device. This event covers all device classes including network adapters, Bluetooth controllers, PCIe cards, USB devices, and other bus-connected hardware, as well as virtual or software-emulated devices. From a BYOVD (Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver) perspective, malicious drivers installed via kernel exploit or signed-driver abuse appear here alongside legitimate driver installations.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service

investigationPivots: DriverProvider not matching a known hardware manufacturer or not matching the DriverName vendor is a BYOVD indicator
Correlate with System EID 7045 — a new service appearing in close temporal proximity to this event indicates the driver loaded as a kernel service (common BYOVD pattern)

DeviceInstanceId containing 'ROOT\' indicates a software-enumerated (non-physical) device — legitimate for virtual devices like VPN adapters, but unusual ROOT\ devices from unknown providers are suspicious
Verify driver digital signature via Get-AuthenticodeSignature or signtool — test-signed drivers on production systems indicate Driver Signature Enforcement bypass
Cross-reference DriverName path with known-good driver paths; drivers installed to Temp, AppData, or other user-writable paths are high-confidence malicious indicators
commonFalsePositives: New legitimate hardware being connected for the first time
Driver updates via Windows Update or vendor update utilities
Virtual network adapters installed by VPN software, hypervisors (VMware, VirtualBox), or Docker
USB devices requiring driver installation on first connection (printer, scanner, webcam)

keyFields.name: DeviceInstanceId
keyFields.description: Full device instance identifier; ROOT\ prefix indicates software/virtual device; encodes VID/PID for physical hardware

keyFields.name: DriverName
keyFields.description: INF file or driver path of the installed driver; unexpected paths in user-writable locations are high risk

keyFields.name: DriverProvider
keyFields.description: Vendor/publisher of the driver; unknown or generic publishers warrant review

keyFields.name: DriverVersion
keyFields.description: Version of the installed driver; test-signed drivers on production systems indicate Driver Signature Enforcement bypass

detectionRules.title: Driver Installed from Non-Standard Path
detectionRules.rule:
Event
| where Source == "Microsoft-Windows-UserPnP"
| where EventID == 20001
| where RenderedDescription has_any ("\\AppData\\", "\\Temp\\", "\\Downloads\\", "\\Public\\")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, RenderedDescription
detectionRules.notes: Legitimate drivers install from Windows driver store or vendor installation directories. Drivers placed in user-writable paths are strong malicious indicators.

detectionRules.title: Device Driver Installed — Suspicious Provider or Path
detectionRules.rule:
title: Suspicious Device Driver Installation
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: system
detection:
    selection:
        Source: 'Microsoft-Windows-UserPnP'
        EventID: 20001
    filter_standard:
        DriverProvider|contains:
            - 'Microsoft'
            - 'Intel'
            - 'NVIDIA'
            - 'Realtek'
            - 'AMD'
            - 'Broadcom'
            - 'Qualcomm'
    condition: selection and not filter_standard
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate third-party hardware drivers from smaller vendors
    - Open-source driver projects
level: medium
detectionRules.notes: Filter list is illustrative. Expand with known driver publishers in your environment. Unknown publishers should be reviewed, not necessarily alerted on blindly.

entries.title: Device driver installation failed
entries.summary: Windows failed to install device drivers for a new hardware device.
entries.details: Generated when Windows cannot successfully install drivers for a detected device. Driver installation failures have two security-relevant scenarios: (1) a legitimate device failing to install due to driver compatibility or signing issues, common after Windows version upgrades; (2) a BYOVD attack where an attacker attempts to load a malicious or vulnerable driver but fails, and this event records the failed attempt. InstallStatus 0xC0000428 (STATUS_INVALID_IMAGE_HASH) indicates a driver signature verification failure, meaning an unsigned or improperly signed driver was blocked by Driver Signature Enforcement.

investigationPivots: Error code 0xC0000428 (STATUS_INVALID_IMAGE_HASH) indicates a blocked unsigned driver — check if the device was a software-enumerated (ROOT\) device which would suggest a driver-loading exploit
Multiple 20003 failures from the same DeviceInstanceId followed by a 20001 success indicates a persistent attacker attempting to load a driver
ROOT\ DeviceInstanceId on a machine without corresponding physical hardware indicates a virtual device being instantiated — common in BYOVD scenarios
Correlate with Sysmon EID 6 (DriverLoad) with Signature = 'Invalid' for a kernel-level view of blocked driver loads
Correlate with Security log Policy Changes subcategory for the full driver installation picture
commonFalsePositives: New hardware with no compatible in-box Windows driver (requires manual driver installation)
Unsigned hardware drivers from hobbyist or industrial equipment without WHQL certification
Test environments with Driver Signature Enforcement disabled generating expected failures when it is re-enabled

keyFields.name: DeviceInstanceId
keyFields.description: Device identifier; ROOT\ prefix on non-physical device is a BYOVD indicator

keyFields.name: DriverName
keyFields.description: Driver INF path that failed to install; examine path and publisher

keyFields.name: InstallStatus
keyFields.description: Error code; 0xC0000428 = invalid image hash (unsigned driver blocked by DSE)
